speaker: TIMON
text: O blessèd breeding sun, draw from the Earth

speaker: TIMON
text: Rotten humidity! Below thy sister’s orb

speaker: TIMON
text: Infect the air! Twinned brothers of one womb,

speaker: TIMON
text: Whose procreation, residence, and birth

speaker: TIMON
text: Scarce is dividant, touch them with several fortunes,

speaker: TIMON
text: The greater scorns the lesser. Not nature,

speaker: TIMON
text: To whom all sores lay siege, can bear great fortune

speaker: TIMON
text: But by contempt of nature.

speaker: TIMON
text: Raise me this beggar, and deny ’t that lord;

speaker: TIMON
text: The Senators shall bear contempt hereditary,

speaker: TIMON
text: The beggar native honor.

speaker: TIMON
text: It is the pasture lards the brother’s sides,

speaker: TIMON
text: The want that makes him lean. Who dares, who dares

speaker: TIMON
text: In purity of manhood stand upright

speaker: TIMON
text: And say “This man’s a flatterer”? If one be,

speaker: TIMON
text: So are they all, for every grise of fortune

speaker: TIMON
text: Is smoothed by that below. The learnèd pate

speaker: TIMON
text: Ducks to the golden fool. All’s obliquy.

speaker: TIMON
text: There’s nothing level in our cursèd natures

speaker: TIMON
text: But direct villainy. Therefore be abhorred

speaker: TIMON
text: All feasts, societies, and throngs of men.

speaker: TIMON
text: His semblable, yea, himself, Timon disdains.

speaker: TIMON
text: Destruction fang mankind! Earth, yield me roots!

speaker: TIMON
text: Who seeks for better of thee, sauce his palate

speaker: TIMON
text: With thy most operant poison!

speaker: TIMON
text: What is here?

speaker: TIMON
text: Gold? Yellow, glittering, precious gold?

speaker: TIMON
text: No, gods, I am no idle votarist.

speaker: TIMON
text: Roots, you clear heavens! Thus much of this will make

speaker: TIMON
text: Black white, foul fair, wrong right,

speaker: TIMON
text: Base noble, old young, coward valiant.

speaker: TIMON
text: Ha, you gods! Why this? What this, you gods? Why, this

speaker: TIMON
text: Will lug your priests and servants from your sides,

speaker: TIMON
text: Pluck stout men’s pillows from below their heads.

speaker: TIMON
text: This yellow slave

speaker: TIMON
text: Will knit and break religions, bless th’ accursed,

speaker: TIMON
text: Make the hoar leprosy adored, place thieves

speaker: TIMON
text: And give them title, knee, and approbation

speaker: TIMON
text: With senators on the bench. This is it

speaker: TIMON
text: That makes the wappened widow wed again;

speaker: TIMON
text: She whom the spital house and ulcerous sores

speaker: TIMON
text: Would cast the gorge at, this embalms and spices

speaker: TIMON
text: To th’ April day again. Come, damnèd earth,

speaker: TIMON
text: Thou common whore of mankind, that puts odds

speaker: TIMON
text: Among the rout of nations, I will make thee

speaker: TIMON
text:
Do thy right nature.

                  afar
                   
                  off

                )
               Ha? A drum? Thou ’rt quick,

speaker: TIMON
text: But yet I’ll bury thee. Thou ’lt go, strong thief,

speaker: TIMON
text: When gouty keepers of thee cannot stand.

speaker: TIMON
text: Nay, stay thou out for earnest.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: What art thou there? Speak.

speaker: TIMON
text: A beast, as thou art. The canker gnaw thy heart

speaker: TIMON
text: For showing me again the eyes of man!

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: What is thy name? Is man so hateful to thee

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: That art thyself a man?

speaker: TIMON
text: I am Misanthropos and hate mankind.

speaker: TIMON
text: For thy part, I do wish thou wert a dog,

speaker: TIMON
text: That I might love thee something.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: I know thee well.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: But in thy fortunes am unlearned and strange.

speaker: TIMON
text: I know thee too, and more than that I know thee

speaker: TIMON
text: I not desire to know. Follow thy drum.

speaker: TIMON
text: With man’s blood paint the ground gules, gules!

speaker: TIMON
text: Religious canons, civil laws are cruel.

speaker: TIMON
text: Then what should war be? This fell whore of thine

speaker: TIMON
text: Hath in her more destruction than thy sword,

speaker: TIMON
text: For all her cherubin look.

speaker: PHRYNIA
text: Thy lips rot off!

speaker: TIMON
text: I will not kiss thee. Then the rot returns

speaker: TIMON
text: To thine own lips again.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: How came the noble Timon to this change?

speaker: TIMON
text: As the moon does, by wanting light to give.

speaker: TIMON
text: But then renew I could not, like the moon;

speaker: TIMON
text: There were no suns to borrow of.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Noble Timon, what friendship may I do thee?

speaker: TIMON
text: None, but to maintain my opinion.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: What is it, Timon?

speaker: TIMON
text: Promise me friendship, but perform none. If

speaker: TIMON
text: thou wilt not promise, the gods plague thee, for

speaker: TIMON
text: thou art a man. If thou dost perform, confound

speaker: TIMON
text: thee, for thou art a man.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: I have heard in some sort of thy miseries.

speaker: TIMON
text: Thou saw’st them when I had prosperity.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: I see them now. Then was a blessèd time.

speaker: TIMON
text: As thine is now, held with a brace of harlots.

speaker: TIMANDRA
text: Is this th’ Athenian minion whom the world

speaker: TIMANDRA
text: Voiced so regardfully?

speaker: TIMON
text: Art thou Timandra?

speaker: TIMANDRA
text: Yes.

speaker: TIMON
text: Be a whore still. They love thee not that use thee.

speaker: TIMON
text: Give them diseases, leaving with thee their lust.

speaker: TIMON
text: Make use of thy salt hours. Season the slaves

speaker: TIMON
text: For tubs and baths. Bring down rose-cheeked youth

speaker: TIMON
text: To the tub-fast and the diet.

speaker: TIMANDRA
text: Hang thee, monster!

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Pardon him, sweet Timandra, for his wits

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Are drowned and lost in his calamities.—

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: I have but little gold of late, brave Timon,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: The want whereof doth daily make revolt

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: In my penurious band. I have heard and grieved

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: How cursèd Athens, mindless of thy worth,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Forgetting thy great deeds when neighbor states,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: But for thy sword and fortune, trod upon them—

speaker: TIMON
text: I prithee, beat thy drum and get thee gone.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: I am thy friend and pity thee, dear Timon.

speaker: TIMON
text: How dost thou pity him whom thou dost trouble?

speaker: TIMON
text: I had rather be alone.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Why, fare thee well. Here is some gold for thee.

speaker: TIMON
text: Keep it. I cannot eat it.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: When I have laid proud Athens on a heap—

speaker: TIMON
text: Warr’st thou ’gainst Athens?

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Ay, Timon, and have cause.

speaker: TIMON
text: The gods confound them all in thy conquest,

speaker: TIMON
text: And thee after, when thou hast conquered!

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Why me, Timon?

speaker: TIMON
text: That by killing of villains

speaker: TIMON
text: Thou wast born to conquer my country.

speaker: TIMON
text: Put up thy gold. Go on. Here’s gold. Go on.

speaker: TIMON
text: Be as a planetary plague when Jove

speaker: TIMON
text: Will o’er some high-viced city hang his poison

speaker: TIMON
text: In the sick air. Let not thy sword skip one.

speaker: TIMON
text: Pity not honored age for his white beard;

speaker: TIMON
text: He is an usurer. Strike me the counterfeit matron;

speaker: TIMON
text: It is her habit only that is honest,

speaker: TIMON
text: Herself’s a bawd. Let not the virgin’s cheek

speaker: TIMON
text: Make soft thy trenchant sword, for those milk paps,

speaker: TIMON
text: That through the window-bars bore at men’s eyes,

speaker: TIMON
text: Are not within the leaf of pity writ,

speaker: TIMON
text: But set them down horrible traitors. Spare not the babe,

speaker: TIMON
text: Whose dimpled smiles from fools exhaust their mercy;

speaker: TIMON
text: Think it a bastard whom the oracle

speaker: TIMON
text: Hath doubtfully pronounced the throat shall cut,

speaker: TIMON
text: And mince it sans remorse. Swear against objects;

speaker: TIMON
text: Put armor on thine ears and on thine eyes,

speaker: TIMON
text: Whose proof nor yells of mothers, maids, nor babes,

speaker: TIMON
text: Nor sight of priests in holy vestments bleeding,

speaker: TIMON
text:
Shall pierce a jot.

                He
                 
                offers
                 
                gold

                )
               There’s gold to pay thy soldiers.

speaker: TIMON
text: Make large confusion and, thy fury spent,

speaker: TIMON
text: Confounded be thyself! Speak not. Begone.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Hast thou gold yet? I’ll take the gold thou givest me,

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Not all thy counsel.

speaker: TIMON
text: Dost thou or dost thou not, heaven’s curse upon thee!

speaker:
BOTH
               
              WOMEN
text: Give us some gold, good Timon. Hast thou more?

speaker: TIMON
text: Enough to make a whore forswear her trade,

speaker: TIMON
text: And to make whores a bawd. Hold up, you sluts,

speaker: TIMON
text: Your aprons mountant.

speaker: TIMON
text: You are not oathable,

speaker: TIMON
text: Although I know you’ll swear—terribly swear

speaker: TIMON
text: Into strong shudders and to heavenly agues

speaker: TIMON
text: Th’ immortal gods that hear you. Spare your oaths.

speaker: TIMON
text: I’ll trust to your conditions. Be whores still.

speaker: TIMON
text: And he whose pious breath seeks to convert you,

speaker: TIMON
text: Be strong in whore, allure him, burn him up.

speaker: TIMON
text: Let your close fire predominate his smoke,

speaker: TIMON
text: And be no turncoats. Yet may your pains six months

speaker: TIMON
text: Be quite contrary. And thatch your poor thin roofs

speaker: TIMON
text: With burdens of the dead—some that were hanged,

speaker: TIMON
text: No matter; wear them, betray with them. Whore still.

speaker: TIMON
text: Paint till a horse may mire upon your face.

speaker: TIMON
text: A pox of wrinkles!

speaker:
BOTH
               
              WOMEN
text: Well, more gold. What then?

speaker:
BOTH
               
              WOMEN
text: Believe ’t that we’ll do anything for gold.

speaker: TIMON
text: Consumptions sow

speaker: TIMON
text: In hollow bones of man; strike their sharp shins,

speaker: TIMON
text: And mar men’s spurring. Crack the lawyer’s voice,

speaker: TIMON
text: That he may never more false title plead

speaker: TIMON
text: Nor sound his quillets shrilly. Hoar the flamen,

speaker: TIMON
text: That scolds against the quality of flesh

speaker: TIMON
text: And not believes himself. Down with the nose—

speaker: TIMON
text: Down with it flat, take the bridge quite away—

speaker: TIMON
text: Of him that, his particular to foresee,

speaker: TIMON
text: Smells from the general weal. Make curled-pate ruffians bald,

speaker: TIMON
text: And let the unscarred braggarts of the war

speaker: TIMON
text: Derive some pain from you. Plague all,

speaker: TIMON
text: That your activity may defeat and quell

speaker: TIMON
text: The source of all erection. There’s more gold.

speaker: TIMON
text: Do you damn others, and let this damn you,

speaker: TIMON
text: And ditches grave you all!

speaker:
BOTH
               
              WOMEN
text: More counsel with more money, bounteous Timon.

speaker: TIMON
text: More whore, more mischief first! I have given you earnest.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Strike up the drum towards Athens.—Farewell, Timon.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: If I thrive well, I’ll visit thee again.

speaker: TIMON
text: If I hope well, I’ll never see thee more.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: I never did thee harm.

speaker: TIMON
text: Yes, thou spok’st well of me.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Call’st thou that harm?

speaker: TIMON
text: Men daily find it. Get thee away, and take

speaker: TIMON
text: Thy beagles with thee.

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: We but offend him.—

speaker: ALCIBIADES
text: Strike.

speaker: TIMON
text: That nature, being sick of man’s unkindness,

speaker: TIMON
text:
Should yet be hungry!
                 
                (
                He
                 
                digs

                )
               Common mother, thou

speaker: TIMON
text: Whose womb unmeasurable and infinite breast

speaker: TIMON
text: Teems and feeds all; whose selfsame mettle—

speaker: TIMON
text: Whereof thy proud child, arrogant man, is puffed—

speaker: TIMON
text: Engenders the black toad and adder blue,

speaker: TIMON
text: The gilded newt and eyeless venomed worm,

speaker: TIMON
text: With all th’ abhorrèd births below crisp heaven

speaker: TIMON
text: Whereon Hyperion’s quick’ning fire doth shine:

speaker: TIMON
text: Yield him who all thy human sons do hate,

speaker: TIMON
text: From forth thy plenteous bosom, one poor root!

speaker: TIMON
text: Ensear thy fertile and conceptious womb;

speaker: TIMON
text: Let it no more bring out ingrateful man.

speaker: TIMON
text: Go great with tigers, dragons, wolves, and bears;

speaker: TIMON
text: Teem with new monsters, whom thy upward face

speaker: TIMON
text: Hath to the marbled mansion all above

speaker: TIMON
text: Never presented. O, a root! Dear thanks!

speaker: TIMON
text: Dry up thy marrows, vines, and plow-torn leas,

speaker: TIMON
text: Whereof ingrateful man with liquorish drafts

speaker: TIMON
text: And morsels unctuous greases his pure mind,

speaker: TIMON
text: That from it all consideration slips—

speaker: TIMON
text: More man? Plague, plague!

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: I was directed hither. Men report

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Thou dost affect my manners and dost use them.

speaker: TIMON
text: ’Tis, then, because thou dost not keep a dog,

speaker: TIMON
text: Whom I would imitate. Consumption catch thee!

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: This is in thee a nature but infected,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: A poor unmanly melancholy sprung

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: From change of future. Why this spade? This place?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: This slavelike habit and these looks of care?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Thy flatterers yet wear silk, drink wine, lie soft,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Hug their diseased perfumes, and have forgot

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: That ever Timon was. Shame not these woods

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: By putting on the cunning of a carper.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Be thou a flatterer now, and seek to thrive

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: By that which has undone thee. Hinge thy knee,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: And let his very breath whom thou ’lt observe

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Blow off thy cap; praise his most vicious strain,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: And call it excellent. Thou wast told thus.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Thou gav’st thine ears, like tapsters that bade welcome,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: To knaves and all approachers. ’Tis most just

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: That thou turn rascal. Had’st thou wealth again,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Rascals should have ’t. Do not assume my likeness.

speaker: TIMON
text: Were I like thee, I’d throw away myself.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Thou hast cast away thyself, being like thyself—

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: A madman so long, now a fool. What, think’st

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: That the bleak air, thy boisterous chamberlain,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Will put thy shirt on warm? Will these moist trees,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: That have outlived the eagle, page thy heels

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: And skip when thou point’st out? Will the cold brook,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Candied with ice, caudle thy morning taste

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: To cure thy o’ernight’s surfeit? Call the creatures

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Whose naked natures live in all the spite

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Of wreakful heaven, whose bare unhousèd trunks,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: To the conflicting elements exposed,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Answer mere nature. Bid them flatter thee.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: O, thou shalt find—

speaker: TIMON
text: A fool of thee. Depart.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: I love thee better now than e’er I did.

speaker: TIMON
text: I hate thee worse.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Why?

speaker: TIMON
text: Thou flatter’st misery.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: I flatter not but say thou art a caitiff.

speaker: TIMON
text: Why dost thou seek me out?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: To vex thee.

speaker: TIMON
text: Always a villain’s office or a fool’s.

speaker: TIMON
text: Dost please thyself in ’t?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Ay.

speaker: TIMON
text: What, a knave too?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: If thou didst put this sour cold habit on

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: To castigate thy pride, ’twere well, but thou

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Dost it enforcedly. Thou ’dst courtier be again

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Wert thou not beggar. Willing misery

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Outlives incertain pomp, is crowned before;

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: The one is filling still, never complete,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: The other at high wish. Best state, contentless,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Hath a distracted and most wretched being,

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Worse than the worst, content.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Thou shouldst desire to die, being miserable.

speaker: TIMON
text: Not by his breath that is more miserable.

speaker: TIMON
text: Thou art a slave whom Fortune’s tender arm

speaker: TIMON
text: With favor never clasped but bred a dog.

speaker: TIMON
text: Hadst thou, like us from our first swathe, proceeded

speaker: TIMON
text: The sweet degrees that this brief world affords

speaker: TIMON
text: To such as may the passive drugs of it

speaker: TIMON
text: Freely command, thou wouldst have plunged thyself

speaker: TIMON
text: In general riot, melted down thy youth

speaker: TIMON
text: In different beds of lust, and never learned

speaker: TIMON
text: The icy precepts of respect, but followed

speaker: TIMON
text: The sugared game before thee. But myself—

speaker: TIMON
text: Who had the world as my confectionary,

speaker: TIMON
text: The mouths, the tongues, the eyes and hearts of men

speaker: TIMON
text: At duty, more than I could frame employment,

speaker: TIMON
text: That numberless upon me stuck as leaves

speaker: TIMON
text: Do on the oak, have with one winter’s brush

speaker: TIMON
text: Fell from their boughs and left me open, bare,

speaker: TIMON
text: For every storm that blows—I to bear this,

speaker: TIMON
text: That never knew but better, is some burden.

speaker: TIMON
text: Thy nature did commence in sufferance. Time

speaker: TIMON
text: Hath made thee hard in ’t. Why shouldst thou hate men?

speaker: TIMON
text: They never flattered thee. What hast thou given?

speaker: TIMON
text: If thou wilt curse, thy father, that poor rag,

speaker: TIMON
text: Must be thy subject, who in spite put stuff

speaker: TIMON
text: To some she-beggar and compounded thee

speaker: TIMON
text: Poor rogue hereditary. Hence, begone.

speaker: TIMON
text: If thou hadst not been born the worst of men,

speaker: TIMON
text: Thou hadst been a knave and flatterer.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Art thou proud yet?

speaker: TIMON
text: Ay, that I am not thee.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: I, that I was no prodigal.

speaker: TIMON
text: I, that I am one now.

speaker: TIMON
text: Were all the wealth I have shut up in thee,

speaker: TIMON
text: I’d give thee leave to hang it. Get thee gone.

speaker: TIMON
text: That the whole life of Athens were in this!

speaker: TIMON
text: Thus would I eat it.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Here, I will mend thy feast.

speaker: TIMON
text: First mend my company. Take away thyself.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: So I shall mend mine own by th’ lack of thine.

speaker: TIMON
text: ’Tis not well mended so; it is but botched.

speaker: TIMON
text: If not, I would it were.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: What wouldst thou have to Athens?

speaker: TIMON
text: Thee thither in a whirlwind. If thou wilt,

speaker: TIMON
text: Tell them there I have gold. Look, so I have.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Here is no use for gold.

speaker: TIMON
text: The best and truest,

speaker: TIMON
text: For here it sleeps and does no hired harm.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Where liest a-nights, Timon?

speaker: TIMON
text: Under that’s above me. Where feed’st thou

speaker: TIMON
text: a-days, Apemantus?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Where my stomach finds meat, or rather

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: where I eat it.

speaker: TIMON
text: Would poison were obedient and knew my

speaker: TIMON
text: mind!

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Where wouldst thou send it?

speaker: TIMON
text: To sauce thy dishes.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: The middle of humanity thou never

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: knewest, but the extremity of both ends. When

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: thou wast in thy gilt and thy perfume, they

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: mocked thee for too much curiosity. In thy rags

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: thou know’st none, but art despised for the contrary.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: There’s a medlar for thee. Eat it.

speaker: TIMON
text: On what I hate I feed not.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Dost hate a medlar?

speaker: TIMON
text: Ay, though it look like thee.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: An thou ’dst hated meddlers sooner, thou

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: shouldst have loved thyself better now. What man

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: didst thou ever know unthrift that was beloved

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: after his means?

speaker: TIMON
text: Who, without those means thou talk’st of, didst

speaker: TIMON
text: thou ever know beloved?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Myself.

speaker: TIMON
text: I understand thee. Thou hadst some means to

speaker: TIMON
text: keep a dog.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: What things in the world canst thou nearest

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: compare to thy flatterers?

speaker: TIMON
text: Women nearest, but men—men are the things

speaker: TIMON
text: themselves. What wouldst thou do with the world,

speaker: TIMON
text: Apemantus, if it lay in thy power?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Give it the beasts, to be rid of the men.

speaker: TIMON
text: Wouldst thou have thyself fall in the confusion

speaker: TIMON
text: of men and remain a beast with the beasts?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Ay, Timon.

speaker: TIMON
text: A beastly ambition, which the gods grant thee

speaker: TIMON
text: t’ attain to! If thou wert the lion, the fox would

speaker: TIMON
text: beguile thee. If thou wert the lamb, the fox would

speaker: TIMON
text: eat thee. If thou wert the fox, the lion would suspect

speaker: TIMON
text: thee when peradventure thou wert accused by

speaker: TIMON
text: the ass. If thou wert the ass, thy dullness would

speaker: TIMON
text: torment thee, and still thou lived’st but as a breakfast

speaker: TIMON
text: to the wolf. If thou wert the wolf, thy greediness

speaker: TIMON
text: would afflict thee, and oft thou shouldst hazard

speaker: TIMON
text: thy life for thy dinner. Wert thou the unicorn,

speaker: TIMON
text: pride and wrath would confound thee and

speaker: TIMON
text: make thine own self the conquest of thy fury. Wert

speaker: TIMON
text: thou a bear, thou wouldst be killed by the horse.

speaker: TIMON
text: Wert thou a horse, thou wouldst be seized by the

speaker: TIMON
text: leopard. Wert thou a leopard, thou wert germane

speaker: TIMON
text: to the lion, and the spots of thy kindred were

speaker: TIMON
text: jurors on thy life. All thy safety were remotion, and

speaker: TIMON
text: thy defense absence. What beast couldst thou be

speaker: TIMON
text: that were not subject to a beast? And what a beast

speaker: TIMON
text: art thou already that seest not thy loss in

speaker: TIMON
text: transformation!

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: If thou couldst please me with speaking to

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: me, thou mightst have hit upon it here. The commonwealth

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: of Athens is become a forest of beasts.

speaker: TIMON
text: How, has the ass broke the wall that thou art

speaker: TIMON
text: out of the city?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Yonder comes a poet and a painter. The

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: plague of company light upon thee! I will fear to

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: catch it and give way. When I know not what else

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: to do, I’ll see thee again.

speaker: TIMON
text: When there is nothing living but thee, thou

speaker: TIMON
text: shalt be welcome. I had rather be a beggar’s dog

speaker: TIMON
text: than Apemantus.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Thou art the cap of all the fools alive.

speaker: TIMON
text: Would thou wert clean enough to spit upon!

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: A plague on thee! Thou art too bad to curse.

speaker: TIMON
text: All villains that do stand by thee are pure.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: There is no leprosy but what thou speak’st.

speaker: TIMON
text: If I name thee.

speaker: TIMON
text: I’ll beat thee, but I should infect my hands.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: I would my tongue could rot them off!

speaker: TIMON
text: Away, thou issue of a mangy dog!

speaker: TIMON
text: Choler does kill me that thou art alive.

speaker: TIMON
text: I swoon to see thee.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Would thou wouldst burst!

speaker: TIMON
text: Away, thou tedious rogue!

speaker: TIMON
text: I am sorry I shall lose a stone by thee.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Beast!

speaker: TIMON
text: Slave!

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Toad!

speaker: TIMON
text: Rogue, rogue, rogue!

speaker: TIMON
text: I am sick of this false world, and will love nought

speaker: TIMON
text: But even the mere necessities upon ’t.

speaker: TIMON
text: Then, Timon, presently prepare thy grave.

speaker: TIMON
text: Lie where the light foam of the sea may beat

speaker: TIMON
text: Thy gravestone daily. Make thine epitaph,

speaker: TIMON
text: That death in me at others’ lives may laugh.

speaker: TIMON
text:
(
                To
                 
                his
                 
                gold
                .
                )
                 
              O thou sweet king-killer and dear divorce

speaker: TIMON
text: ’Twixt natural son and sire, thou bright defiler

speaker: TIMON
text: Of Hymen’s purest bed, thou valiant Mars,

speaker: TIMON
text: Thou ever young, fresh, loved, and delicate wooer,

speaker: TIMON
text: Whose blush doth thaw the consecrated snow

speaker: TIMON
text: That lies on Dian’s lap; thou visible god,

speaker: TIMON
text: That sold’rest close impossibilities

speaker: TIMON
text: And mak’st them kiss, that speak’st with every tongue

speaker: TIMON
text: To every purpose! O thou touch of hearts,

speaker: TIMON
text: Think thy slave, man, rebels, and by thy virtue

speaker: TIMON
text: Set them into confounding odds, that beasts

speaker: TIMON
text: May have the world in empire!

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Would ’twere so!

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: But not till I am dead. I’ll say thou ’st gold;

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Thou wilt be thronged to shortly.

speaker: TIMON
text: Thronged to?

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Ay.

speaker: TIMON
text: Thy back, I prithee.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: Live and love thy misery.

speaker: TIMON
text: Long live so, and so die. I am quit.

speaker: APEMANTUS
text: More things like men.—Eat, Timon, and abhor them.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: Where should he have this gold? It is

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: some poor fragment, some slender ort of his

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: remainder. The mere want of gold and the falling-from

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: of his friends drove him into this melancholy.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BANDIT
text: It is noised he hath a mass of treasure.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              BANDIT
text: Let us make the assay upon him. If he

speaker:
THIRD
               
              BANDIT
text: care not for ’t, he will supply us easily. If he covetously

speaker:
THIRD
               
              BANDIT
text: reserve it, how shall ’s get it?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BANDIT
text: True, for he bears it not about him. ’Tis

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BANDIT
text: hid.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: Is not this he?

speaker: OTHERS
text: Where?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BANDIT
text: ’Tis his description.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              BANDIT
text: He. I know him.

speaker: ALL
text: Save thee, Timon.

speaker: TIMON
text: Now, thieves?

speaker: ALL
text: Soldiers, not thieves.

speaker: TIMON
text: Both, too, and women’s sons.

speaker: ALL
text: We are not thieves, but men that much do want.

speaker: TIMON
text: Your greatest want is, you want much of meat.

speaker: TIMON
text: Why should you want? Behold, the earth hath roots.

speaker: TIMON
text: Within this mile break forth a hundred springs.

speaker: TIMON
text: The oaks bear mast, the briars scarlet hips.

speaker: TIMON
text: The bounteous huswife Nature on each bush

speaker: TIMON
text: Lays her full mess before you. Want? Why want?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: We cannot live on grass, on berries, water,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: As beasts and birds and fishes.

speaker: TIMON
text: Nor on the beasts themselves, the birds and fishes;

speaker: TIMON
text: You must eat men. Yet thanks I must you con

speaker: TIMON
text: That you are thieves professed, that you work not

speaker: TIMON
text: In holier shapes, for there is boundless theft

speaker: TIMON
text: In limited professions. Rascal thieves,

speaker: TIMON
text:
Here’s gold.
                 
                (
                He
                 
                gives
                 
                them
                 
                gold
                .
                )
               Go, suck the subtle blood o’ th’ grape

speaker: TIMON
text: Till the high fever seethe your blood to froth,

speaker: TIMON
text: And so ’scape hanging. Trust not the physician;

speaker: TIMON
text: His antidotes are poison, and he slays

speaker: TIMON
text: More than you rob. Take wealth and lives together.

speaker: TIMON
text: Do, villainy, do, since you protest to do ’t,

speaker: TIMON
text: Like workmen. I’ll example you with thievery.

speaker: TIMON
text: The sun’s a thief and with his great attraction

speaker: TIMON
text: Robs the vast sea. The moon’s an arrant thief,

speaker: TIMON
text: And her pale fire she snatches from the sun.

speaker: TIMON
text: The sea’s a thief, whose liquid surge resolves

speaker: TIMON
text: The moon into salt tears. The earth’s a thief,

speaker: TIMON
text: That feeds and breeds by a composture stol’n

speaker: TIMON
text: From gen’ral excrement. Each thing’s a thief.

speaker: TIMON
text: The laws, your curb and whip, in their rough power

speaker: TIMON
text: Has unchecked theft. Love not yourselves. Away!

speaker: TIMON
text: Rob one another. There’s more gold.

speaker: TIMON
text: Cut throats.

speaker: TIMON
text: All that you meet are thieves. To Athens go.

speaker: TIMON
text: Break open shops. Nothing can you steal

speaker: TIMON
text: But thieves do lose it. Steal less for this I give you,

speaker: TIMON
text: And gold confound you howsoe’er! Amen.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              BANDIT
text: Has almost charmed me from my profession

speaker:
THIRD
               
              BANDIT
text: by persuading me to it.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: ’Tis in the malice of mankind that he

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: thus advises us, not to have us thrive in our

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: mystery.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BANDIT
text: I’ll believe him as an enemy and give

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BANDIT
text: over my trade.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: Let us first see peace in Athens. There is

speaker:
FIRST
               
              BANDIT
text: no time so miserable but a man may be true.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: O you gods!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Is yond despised and ruinous man my lord?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Full of decay and flailing? O, monument

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: And wonder of good deeds evilly bestowed!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: What an alteration of honor has desp’rate want made!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: What viler thing upon the Earth than friends,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Who can bring noblest minds to basest ends!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: How rarely does it meet with this time’s guise,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: When man was wished to love his enemies!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Grant I may ever love, and rather woo

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Those that would mischief me than those that do!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Has caught me in his eye. I will present

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: My honest grief unto him and as my lord

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Still serve him with my life.—My dearest master.

speaker: TIMON
text: Away! What art thou?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Have you forgot me, sir?

speaker: TIMON
text: Why dost ask that? I have forgot all men.

speaker: TIMON
text: Then, if thou grant’st thou ’rt a man, I have forgot thee.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: An honest poor servant of yours.

speaker: TIMON
text: Then I know thee not.

speaker: TIMON
text: I never had honest man about me, I. All

speaker: TIMON
text: I kept were knaves to serve in meat to villains.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: The gods are witness,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Ne’er did poor steward wear a truer grief

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: For his undone lord than mine eyes for you.

speaker: TIMON
text: What, dost thou weep? Come nearer, then. I love thee

speaker: TIMON
text: Because thou art a woman and disclaim’st

speaker: TIMON
text: Flinty mankind, whose eyes do never give

speaker: TIMON
text: But thorough lust and laughter. Pity’s sleeping.

speaker: TIMON
text: Strange times that weep with laughing, not with weeping!

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: I beg of you to know me, good my lord,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: T’ accept my grief, and, whilst this poor wealth lasts,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: To entertain me as your steward still.

speaker: TIMON
text: Had I a steward

speaker: TIMON
text: So true, so just, and now so comfortable?

speaker: TIMON
text: It almost turns my dangerous nature mild.

speaker: TIMON
text: Let me behold thy face. Surely this man

speaker: TIMON
text: Was born of woman.

speaker: TIMON
text: Forgive my general and exceptless rashness,

speaker: TIMON
text: You perpetual-sober gods. I do proclaim

speaker: TIMON
text: One honest man—mistake me not, but one;

speaker: TIMON
text: No more, I pray!—and he’s a steward.

speaker: TIMON
text: How fain would I have hated all mankind,

speaker: TIMON
text: And thou redeem’st thyself. But all, save thee,

speaker: TIMON
text: I fell with curses.

speaker: TIMON
text: Methinks thou art more honest now than wise,

speaker: TIMON
text: For by oppressing and betraying me

speaker: TIMON
text: Thou mightst have sooner got another service;

speaker: TIMON
text: For many so arrive at second masters

speaker: TIMON
text: Upon their first lord’s neck. But tell me true—

speaker: TIMON
text: For I must ever doubt, though ne’er so sure—

speaker: TIMON
text: Is not thy kindness subtle, covetous,

speaker: TIMON
text: A usuring kindness, and as rich men deal gifts,

speaker: TIMON
text: Expecting in return twenty for one?

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: No, my most worthy master, in whose breast

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Doubt and suspect, alas, are placed too late.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: You should have feared false times when you did feast.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Suspect still comes where an estate is least.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: That which I show, heaven knows, is merely love,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Duty, and zeal to your unmatchèd mind,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Care of your food and living. And believe it,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: My most honored lord,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: For any benefit that points to me,

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: Either in hope or present, I’d exchange

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: For this one wish, that you had power and wealth

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: To requite me by making rich yourself.

speaker: TIMON
text: Look thee, ’tis so. Thou singly honest man,

speaker: TIMON
text:
Here, take.
                 
                (
                Timon
                 
                offers
                 
                gold
                .
                )
               The gods out of my misery

speaker: TIMON
text: Has sent thee treasure. Go, live rich and happy,

speaker: TIMON
text: But thus conditioned: thou shalt build from men;

speaker: TIMON
text: Hate all, curse all, show charity to none,

speaker: TIMON
text: But let the famished flesh slide from the bone

speaker: TIMON
text: Ere thou relieve the beggar; give to dogs

speaker: TIMON
text: What thou deniest to men; let prisons swallow ’em,

speaker: TIMON
text: Debts wither ’em to nothing; be men like blasted woods,

speaker: TIMON
text: And may diseases lick up their false bloods!

speaker: TIMON
text: And so farewell and thrive.

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: O, let me stay

speaker: FLAVIUS
text: And comfort you, my master.

speaker: TIMON
text: If thou hat’st curses,

speaker: TIMON
text: Stay not. Fly whilst thou art blest and free.

speaker: TIMON
text: Ne’er see thou man, and let me ne’er see thee.
